title: Ambient operator calculus
summary: Packages a formal calculus of Q, the Euler operator X, and the ambient Laplacian, then proves the resulting homogeneity and commutator consequences under an explicit interface of algebraic identities.

mathematicalContent.heading: Formal ambient calculus
mathematicalContent.body: The module works with all real-valued functions on the formal ambient bundle. A Calculus supplies a defining function Q, an infinitesimal dilation operator X, and an operator denoted by the ambient Laplacian. These are abstract operations; the file does not construct them from the straight-form metric data.
mathematicalContent.formula: Q=|X|_{\widetilde g}^{2},\qquad X=\left.\frac{d}{ds}\right|_{s=0}\delta_{e^s}^{*},\qquad \widetilde\Delta:\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G})\to\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G}).

mathematicalContent.heading: Interface identities and consequences
mathematicalContent.body: AlgebraicIdentities assumes linearity of the formal Laplacian together with the standard ambient commutators. All later theorems in this file are genuine Lean consequences of that interface, including the fact that each application of the Laplacian lowers X-homogeneity by two.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Function is the space of arbitrary real-valued functions on the formal ambient bundle; no smoothness condition is encoded.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev Function : Type _

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zero ambient function vanishes at every ambient point.
correspondence.leanType: def zero : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Addition of ambient functions is defined pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def add (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Subtraction of ambient functions is defined pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def sub (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Multiplication of ambient functions is defined pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def mul (f g : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A real scalar acts on an ambient function pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def smul (c : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Natural powers of an ambient function are taken pointwise.
correspondence.leanType: def pow (f : Function Conf) (m : ℕ) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A Calculus packages straight-form data, a formal dimension n, a function Q, the Euler operator X, and a formal ambient Laplacian. No differential-geometric relation among these objects is part of this structure alone.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \left(\widetilde g,\ n,\ Q,\ X,\ \widetilde\Delta\right),\qquad Q\in\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G}),\quad X,\widetilde\Delta:\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G})\to\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G}).
correspondence.leanType: structure Calculus

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This accessor returns the real parameter stored as the base dimension.
correspondence.leanType: def n : ℝ

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Qmul is multiplication by the defining function Q.
correspondence.leanType: def Qmul (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: QpowMul multiplies an ambient function by the m-th pointwise power of Q.
correspondence.leanType: def QpowMul (m : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A function vanishes modulo Q precisely when it is globally represented as a multiple of Q.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: f=0\pmod Q\quad:\Longleftrightarrow\quad\exists h\in\mathcal F(\widetilde{\mathcal G}),\ f=Qh.
correspondence.leanType: def VanishesModQ (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Two ambient functions are equal modulo Q when their difference is divisible by Q.
correspondence.leanType: def EqModQ (f g : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Equality modulo Q is reflexive because f − f = Q·0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem EqModQ_refl (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.EqModQ f f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: lap is notation for the formal Laplacian supplied by the Calculus structure.
correspondence.leanType: def lap (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The r-th iterate of the formal ambient Laplacian is defined recursively.
correspondence.leanType: def lapPow (r : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zeroth iterate of the ambient Laplacian is the identity operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_zero (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow 0 f = f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The successor recursion applies one further ambient Laplacian.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_succ (r : ℕ) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow (r + 1) f = CalConf.lap (CalConf.lapPow r f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: An ambient function has infinitesimal homogeneity w when it is an eigenfunction of the Euler operator X with eigenvalue w.
correspondence.leanType: def IsXHomogeneous (w : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For a real-valued equivariant ambient density d, this predicate applies the equation Xf = wf to its underlying function.
correspondence.leanType: def IsXHomogeneousDensity {χ : Weight ℝ} (w : ℝ) (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This forgetful map returns the real-valued function underlying an ambient density.
correspondence.leanType: def densityToFunction (_CalConf : Calculus Conf) {χ : Weight ℝ} (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : Function Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluating the forgotten underlying function gives exactly the original density value.
correspondence.leanType: theorem densityToFunction_apply {χ : Weight ℝ} (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.densityToFunction d U = d U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The density predicate is definitionally equivalent to X-homogeneity of the underlying ambient function.
correspondence.leanType: theorem IsXHomogeneousDensity_iff {χ : Weight ℝ} (w : ℝ) (d : Ambient.Equivariant.Density (Conf := Conf) χ) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneousDensity w d ↔ CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w d.toFun

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This proposition-valued interface assumes the standard homogeneity, linearity, and commutator identities required by the later formal proofs. The identities are fields of H, not theorems derived here from a metric.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \begin{aligned}XQ&=2Q,\\ \widetilde\Delta(cf)&=c\widetilde\Delta f,\quad \widetilde\Delta(f+g)=\widetilde\Delta f+\widetilde\Delta g,\\ X\widetilde\Delta f&=\widetilde\Delta Xf-2\widetilde\Delta f,\\ \widetilde\Delta(Qf)&=Q\widetilde\Delta f+2\bigl(2Xf+(n+2)f\bigr),\\ Xf=wf&\Longrightarrow\widetilde\Delta(Q^mf)=Q^m\widetilde\Delta f+2m(2w+n+2m)Q^{m-1}f.\end{aligned}
correspondence.leanType: structure AlgebraicIdentities : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under AlgebraicIdentities, the formal ambient Laplacian commutes with multiplication by a real constant.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_smul (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (c : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => c * f U) = fun U => c * CalConf.lap f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Under AlgebraicIdentities, the formal ambient Laplacian is additive.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_add (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => f U + g U) = fun U => CalConf.lap f U + CalConf.lap g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The interface states that Q is homogeneous of degree two under the Euler operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem X_Q (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) : CalConf.X CalConf.Q = fun U => 2 * CalConf.Q U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The Euler operator and formal ambient Laplacian satisfy the weight-lowering commutator relation.
correspondence.leanType: theorem X_lap_comm (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.X (CalConf.lap f) = fun U => CalConf.lap (CalConf.X f) U - 2 * CalConf.lap f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The basic ambient commutator expands the Laplacian of Qf into Q times the Laplacian of f plus the Euler and dimension terms.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_Q_comm (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (CalConf.Qmul f) = fun U => CalConf.Q U * CalConf.lap f U + 2 * (2 * CalConf.X f U + (CalConf.n + 2) * f U)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If f has X-weight w, the interface supplies the closed formula for applying one ambient Laplacian to Q^m f.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_Qpow_of_XHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (m : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.lap (CalConf.QpowMul m f) = fun U => CalConf.Q U ^ m * CalConf.lap f U + 2 * (m : ℝ) * (2 * w + CalConf.n + 2 * (m : ℝ)) * CalConf.Q U ^ (m - 1) * f U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Linearity implies that the formal ambient Laplacian preserves subtraction.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_sub (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lap (fun U => f U - g U) = fun U => CalConf.lap f U - CalConf.lap g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Every natural power of the formal ambient Laplacian preserves subtraction.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_sub (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) (f g : Function Conf) : CalConf.lapPow k (fun U => f U - g U) = fun U => CalConf.lapPow k f U - CalConf.lapPow k g U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: One application of the ambient Laplacian lowers X-homogeneity by two.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lap_isXHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous (w - 2) (CalConf.lap f)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: This predicate says that the r-th ambient Laplacian of f has the expected weight w − 2r.
correspondence.leanType: def LapPowHasWeight (r : ℕ) (w : ℝ) (f : Function Conf) : Prop

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Iterating the commutator proves that each of r Laplacians lowers the input weight by two.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_isXHomogeneous (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (r : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {f : Function Conf} (hf : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w f) : CalConf.LapPowHasWeight r w f

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For an X-homogeneous h, the iterated commutator formula separates the term still divisible by Q from the boundary contribution after k Laplacians.
correspondence.leanType: theorem lapPow_Qmul_formula (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (k : ℕ) {w : ℝ} {h : Function Conf} (hh : CalConf.IsXHomogeneous w h) : CalConf.lapPow k (CalConf.Qmul h) = fun U => CalConf.Q U * CalConf.lapPow k h U + 2 * (k : ℝ) * (2 * w + CalConf.n + 2 - 2 * (((k - 1 : ℕ) : ℝ))) * CalConf.lapPow (k - 1) h U

references.locator: Chapter 3, straight ambient metrics and the Q–X–Δ̃ commutator calculus
references.note: Geometric source for the displayed homogeneity and commutator identities.

limitations: The Lean consequences are verified only under the AlgebraicIdentities interface. The module does not construct Q, X, or the ambient Laplacian from an actual metric, and it assumes the Q-power formula as a field of that interface. Function also denotes arbitrary functions, not smooth functions.
